news.title: Construction & land deals banned in parts of Gaya till March 2027
news.para: Construction and land transactions are banned in parts of Gaya district until March 31, 2027, to facilitate a satellite township and master plan. The restrictions impact 20 wards in Bodh Gaya Nagar Parishad and 80 revenue villages, aiming to curb haphazard development and guide urban expansion.

news.title: Over 490 plots up for grabs in Lucknow development body's Anantnagar scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is opening the second phase of online registration for 498 residential plots in its Anantnagar scheme from April 28 to May 27. Allotment will be via lottery, with applicants needing to pay a 5% registration fee. This 785-acre project on Mohan Road features a grid layout, wide roads, and underground power lines.

news.title: Gurugram: HSVP puts on hold demolition drive, says awaiting HC instructions
news.para: Gurugram's anti-encroachment drive is paused. Haryana Shahari Vikas Pradhikaran awaits High Court guidance. The court is hearing a petition on stilt-plus-four-floor construction. This policy is linked to many encroachment cases. The drive aims to clear public land of illegal structures. It will resume after clear court orders are received.

news.title: Nashik civic body starts process to cancel 26 tentative layouts linked to MHADA scam
news.para: Nashik Municipal Corporation is cancelling 26 tentative housing layouts. This action follows a probe into the MHADA housing scam. Police have booked 197 individuals. A special investigation team is also looking into the matter. Land records show these layouts are not on official base maps. This move aims to address violations of housing norms.

news.title: Johnson Lifts takes majority control of Toshiba JV, targets doubling of topline
news.para: Johnson Lifts has acquired a majority stake in its joint venture with Toshiba Elevator Corporation, increasing its holding to over 80%. This strategic move positions the company for expansion in India's premium elevator segment, leveraging Toshiba's technology for high-rise buildings. The joint venture aims to double its revenue and increase market share in the growing Indian elevator market.

news.title: ICICI Pru Alternatives buys RMZ office assets in Bengaluru, Pune for ₹2,600 crore
news.para: ICICI Prudential Alternatives has made a significant investment by purchasing two prestigious office buildings in Bengaluru and Pune for roughly Rs 2,600 crore, using funds from the ICICI Prudential Office Yield Optimiser Fund. Both properties are gems in India's commercial landscape, with EcoWorld 21 perched on Bengaluru's Outer Ring Road and RMZ Edge in the vibrant Koregaon Park of Pune.

news.title: File report on illegal buildings in a month, NGT tells Greater Noida authority
news.para: Illegal constructions persist in Noida as the Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority (GNIDA) repeatedly fails to comply with National Green Tribunal (NGT) orders. Despite multiple affidavits and directives, GNIDA has been granted another four weeks to provide crucial information regarding constructions exceeding 5,000 sqm and lacking necessary environmental clearances.

news.title: Noida: Residents of Mahagun Mywoods protest builder’s inaction over safety, infra failures
news.para: Security has emerged as a particular flashpoint, with tower receptions and basement areas left unguarded because the builder has not cleared payments to security agencies

news.title: Malls can’t charge for parking: Consumer court calls it unfair trade practice
news.para: Raipur's consumer commission ruled private malls cannot charge for parking, deeming it an unfair trade practice. The ruling followed a complaint where a visitor was charged Rs 30 to exit Ambuja Mall. The commission cited legal precedents, stating parking is a mandatory component of commercial building approvals and its use should be free for visitors.

news.title: Bihar to develop 11 satellite townships under land-pooling model: Official
news.para: Bihar plans to establish 11 satellite townships utilizing a land-pooling framework from the Town Planning Scheme. This approach ensures that farmers gain back 55 percent of the land after development. The goal is to cultivate well-planned urban spaces that enhance economic development and job creation.

news.title: Bajaj Housing Finance's  profit grows 14% to ₹669 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: Bajaj Housing Finance's net profit surged by 14 percent to Rs 669 crore in Q4 FY26. Total income also saw an increase. The company's asset quality improved with declining gross non-performing assets. Assets under management reached Rs 1.4 lakh crore, marking a significant year-on-year growth.

news.title: The Phoenix Mills' net profit rises 19.60% in FY26
news.para: The Phoenix Mills's net consolidated profit saw a significant increase of 19.60 percent for FY26. Total income also grew by 15.87 percent. The board has recommended a final dividend of ₹2.50 per equity share. In the fourth quarter of FY26, income rose by 21.94 percent and profit after tax increased substantially.

news.title: Embassy Office Parks REIT's net operating income up 15% in FY26
news.para: Embassy REIT achieved a 15 percent growth in net operating income to ₹3,760 crore for FY26. The company also reported a 11.83 percent increase in total income. Distributions for the quarter ending March 31, 2026, were declared. Embassy REIT anticipates double-digit growth in distributions and NOI for FY27.

news.title: Land deals dip 22% across India in FY26: Anarock
news.para: Listed real estate developers accounted for nearly 49% of land deals in FY26, closing 54 transactions across 1,433 acres. Overall, 111 land deals covering about 2,994 acres were recorded during the year, with Bengaluru emerging as the top market.

news.title: UltraTech Cement's net profit rises 35.58% in FY26
news.para: In Q4 FY26, UltraTech Cement's net consolidated total income grew by 11.75 per cent and stood at ₹25,887.03 crore as against ₹23,165.45 crore in the similar quarter last year. Its PAT stood at ₹3,000.02 crore in Q4 FY26 as against ₹2,474.79 crore in Q4 FY25, registering a growth of 21.22 per cent.

news.title: L&T Realty acquires 20 acres land in Gurugram
news.para: L&T Realty Properties has acquired International Green Scapes in Delhi. This move gives LTRPL 20 acres of land in Gurugram. The site has development potential for 3.6 million sq ft. LTRPL and L&T Realty Developers have also completed land acquisitions in Mumbai, Delhi, and Bengaluru. These acquisitions offer a combined development potential of three million sq ft.

news.title: Villagers oppose commercial, multi-dwelling units at Loliem
news.para: Loliem-Polem villagers unanimously opposed large construction projects, including 36 villas, citing concerns over development. The gram sabha urged the panchayat to prevent mega projects and ensure timely desilting of rivulets and drainage cleaning before the monsoons. A proposal for a dog shelter was deemed unfeasible due to resource limitations.

news.title: Delhi: MCD activates Swagam portal for house regularisation in unauthorised colonies
news.para: Delhi residents in 1,511 unauthorized colonies can now apply for property regularization via the MCD's Swagam portal. The civic body has empanelled architects to assist applicants, streamlining the process for obtaining regularisation certificates. Approvals are on an 'as-is-where-is' basis, with penal charges for excess construction. Awareness camps are being organized to guide residents.

news.title: Mumbai: No medical bail to Mid-City Heights' partner in ₹56 crore real estate fraud
news.para: A Mumbai special court has denied medical bail to businessman Amarjeet Shukla, accused in a Rs 55.71 crore real estate fraud. The court found his medical condition, while needing treatment, did not constitute a life-threatening emergency justifying release from custody. Shukla sought bail for surgery, but the court deemed state facilities adequate.

news.title: Mumbai: In a first, SRA starts freezing builders’ sale flats to secure tenants’ rent
news.para: The Slum Rehabilitation Authority (SRA) is now freezing sale flats equivalent to three years' rent to ensure eligible slum dwellers receive their payments and new homes. This measure aims to prevent developers from defaulting on transit rent obligations. The SRA is also pushing for faster project completion and has collected over Rs 1,000 crore in rent.
